programmes.show_description: Phil Williams sits in, live through the night from the Salford Riviera with Midnight Mastermind, Wish You Were There, Midnight Caller and a huge Ravio 2 to end the week.

programmes.show_name: Dance Sounds of the 90s with Vernon Kay
programmes.show_description: Get your horns out and party along to an hour of brass bangers mixed up by Vernon Kay in celebration of Radio 2 Loves Brass. Expect tunes from RuPaul, Rozalla and Wildchild.
Welcome! It's Early Breakfast with Owain Wyn Evans, featuring Step Back in Time and Airdrum Anthems. This is your weekend warm up! What are you looking forward to this weekend?
It's a musicals special with Tom Allen, Claire Sweeney and Michael Ball in the studio!
Actress Holliday Grainger selects her final two Tracks Of My Years of the week and two listeners take on our quiz, Ten To The Top.
Jeremy and guests discuss the news headlines and talk to the people making them.
Trevor is bringing you some of the best music from across the decades to get you through the afternoon.
It's All Request Friday and Sara wants to hear from you on 88291 or sara.cox@bbc.co.uk.
Tonight's gonna be a good night so get into the groove at Michelle's Fabulous Friday party.
Michelle's Mood is... HOME SWEET HOME! Your weekly 30 minutes of non-stop bops right here.
Sophie's ready with a Kitchen Disco to celebrate longer days and lighter nights! There's music from Orson, Kelis, Janet Jackson, Frankie Knuckles and Donna Summer this week.

programmes.show_name: The Rock Show with Shaun Keaveny
programmes.show_description: Mogwai guitarist and co-founder Stuart Braithwaite reveals his Rock God.

programmes.show_name: Romesh Ranganathan: For the Love of Hip Hop
programmes.show_description: Romesh is joined by Kae Tempest, a poet, rapper and playwright from London. They share three songs that have shaped their journey so far, plus a pick from their latest album.
Tony spins super Soul music and celebrates 60 years of Percy Sledge's 'When A Man Loves A Woman', plus 50 years of Shalamar and music from Thelma Jones, Olivia Dean and Bruno Mars.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1983.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1994.
Happy Mondays performing at the Kilburn National in 1992. Featuring Wrote for Luck, Loose Fit, Kinky Afro, Hallelujah and Monkey in the Family.

programmes.show_name: The Unwinds Mix with Angela Griffin
programmes.show_description: Angela Griffin has great British artists like All Saints, Sade and The Style Council, with songs that sound perfect in the sun.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 60s with Tony Blackburn
programmes.show_description: Tony brings you two hours of music from the best decade ever, with hits from Tom Jones, Jose Feliciano and Neil Diamond. This week, the top three comes from 1967.
Phil Dunster chats about his new comedy series with Steve Carrell called Rooster. Plus, some Mercury Rev on vinyl, a wedding call and The Mystery Voice.
Romesh brings the laughs, soundtracked by great music. As well as chatting to brilliant guests, he's sharing your hilarious stories, so you can start your Saturday with a smile!
Spend Saturday lunchtime with Emma and enjoy a menu full of your favourite music, great conversation and a delicious dive into the BBC archives for dessert.
Radio 1 presenters Matt and Mollie pop in to talk about their new podcast, Novel Idea. Plus, Rylan gets an exclusive play of Matt Cardle's new song and sings with him in KaRYoke.
Shaun Keaveny with fabulous teatime tunes and your tales of the extraordinary and the everyday.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 80s with Gary Davies
programmes.show_description: Rock hero Sammy Hagar shares his favourite 80s Moments, we celebrate Salt of Salt-N-Pepa's birthday with a Queens Of Rap Mastermix and T'Pau's Carol Decker joins Gary in Hour 2.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 90s with Fearne Cotton
programmes.show_description: Join Fearne for a nostalgia drenched celebration of the best 90s music and pop culture.
